title: Unit 04 Pretest
description: Diagnostic pretest for Professional Communication and Resume Development.

choices.text: Communication that is clear, respectful, and appropriate for formal settings
choices.feedback: Correct. Professional communication should be clear, respectful, and appropriate for school or workplace settings.

choices.text: Communication that uses as much slang as possible
choices.feedback: Professional communication should avoid slang and overly casual language.

choices.text: Communication that ignores the audience
choices.feedback: Professional communication should consider the audience and purpose.

choices.text: Communication used only with close friends
choices.feedback: Professional communication is meant for formal situations, not only personal conversations.

questions.prompt: A resume often includes sections such as education and skills.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: Resumes often include sections such as education, skills, and experience or activities.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. Education and skills are common resume sections.

questions.prompt: Why does formatting matter on a resume?

choices.text: Because formatting helps organize information clearly
choices.feedback: This is the right idea, but the strongest answer in this set is the one that more directly explains readability and organization.

choices.text: Because formatting makes the resume easier to read and understand
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer here is the one that more directly connects formatting to professional organization.

choices.text: Because formatting helps present information in a clear and organized way
choices.feedback: Correct. Good formatting helps a resume look organized and easy to read.

choices.text: Because formatting replaces the need for relevant content
choices.feedback: Formatting helps presentation, but strong content is still necessary.

questions.prompt: A student writes, 'hey, i want this job because it sounds cool.' What is the biggest communication problem?

choices.text: The message is too formal
choices.feedback: The message is actually too informal, not too formal.

choices.text: The message includes too many section headings
choices.feedback: The issue is tone, not section headings.

choices.text: The message uses an overly casual tone for a professional setting
choices.feedback: Correct. Professional communication should use a more formal and respectful tone.

choices.text: The message is too long
choices.feedback: The main issue is the informal tone, not message length.

questions.prompt: A cover letter is used to explain interest and qualifications for an opportunity.

choices.text: True
choices.feedback: Correct. A cover letter helps explain interest and qualifications in a professional way.

choices.text: False
choices.feedback: A cover letter does help explain interest and qualifications.

questions.prompt: Which detail would be most appropriate in a student resume?

choices.text: A list of favorite snacks
choices.feedback: Favorite snacks are not relevant resume content.

choices.text: Relevant skills, school information, and activities
choices.feedback: Correct. A student resume should include relevant skills, education, and activities or experience.

choices.text: A casual message to friends
choices.feedback: A casual message is not resume content.

choices.text: Unrelated personal opinions
choices.feedback: A resume should focus on relevant and professional information.

questions.prompt: What should a strong application submission show?

choices.text: That the student can follow directions and submit complete professional materials
choices.feedback: Correct. Strong application submissions show completeness, professionalism, and attention to directions.

choices.text: That the student can submit materials that are complete and professional
choices.feedback: This is the right idea, but the strongest answer in this set is the one that more directly emphasizes completeness and professionalism together.

choices.text: That the student can avoid revising anything
choices.feedback: Revision is often an important part of preparing strong documents.

choices.text: That the student can write without considering the opportunity
choices.feedback: Applications should match the purpose of the opportunity.

questions.prompt: Which action best supports professional document quality?

choices.text: Ignoring spelling and organization
choices.feedback: Document quality depends on clear and organized writing.

choices.text: Submitting without checking for completeness
choices.feedback: Students should review documents before submitting them.

choices.text: Using clear writing, organized structure, and careful review
choices.feedback: This is close, but the strongest answer in this set is the one that most directly emphasizes revision before submission.

choices.text: Reviewing documents for clarity, organization, and completeness before submission
choices.feedback: Correct. Reviewing documents before submission helps improve professionalism and quality.
